key_specs.notes: This command allows both access and modification of the key
We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RW and UPDATE
We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RO and ACCESS
We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RW and UPDATE
We cannot tell how the keys will be used so we assume the worst, RO and ACCESS
RW and UPDATE because it changes the TTL

arguments.name: slotsrange-token
arguments.description: One or more ranges of slots to be migrated.

arguments.name: range
arguments.description: Start and end slot for single range

arguments.name: node-id
arguments.description: 40 character node name of the node to migrate to

key_specs.notes: RW because it may change the internal representation of the key, and propagate to replicas
RW and ACCESS due to the optional `GET` argument

arguments.name: comparison-value
arguments.summary: Sets the key's value only if the current value matches the specified comparison value.

key_specs.notes: For the optional BY/GET keyword. It is marked 'unknown' because the key names derive from the content of the key we sort
For the optional STORE keyword. It is marked 'unknown' because the keyword can appear anywhere in the argument array
For the optional BY/GET keyword. It is marked 'unknown' because the key names derive from the content of the key we sort
UPDATE instead of INSERT because of the optional trimming feature
